Good Remedy / GRMove

AI is everywhere now. The hard part is knowing what should move.

Good Remedy helps teams turn AI assisted and review-heavy work into a Move Pack, so leaders can see what is supported, what is missing, who must review, and what the next responsible action is.

See the buyer pains What a Move Pack shows

Make work reviewable before it moves.

One work object. One controlled readout. One clearer next decision.

Not certification. The packet prepares the work.
Not autopilot. Proper authority decides what moves.
Not more noise. The next safe action becomes visible.

The risky moment is not generation. It is movement.

Good Remedy makes work reviewable before it moves.

Where buyers feel it

The pain is not “we need more AI.” It is “can this move?”

Good Remedy starts where the pressure shows up: a draft, deck, packet, campaign, update, or workflow is close to movement, but nobody is fully comfortable letting it move.

Campaign

The work looks finished. The room still hesitates.

The ad is polished. The claim is persuasive. But nobody can quickly say what is supported, what is risky, and who owns the launch decision.

Board update

The vendor AI sounds certain. The team is not.

The recommendation may be useful, but it mixes fact, forecast, assumption, and action language right before leadership sees it.

Prompt loop

Everyone asks for one more version.

The problem is not the wording anymore. It is a missing source, owner, reviewer, or decision rule.

Client email

The note reads clean. The risk is still unowned.

A polished reply can still overpromise, accept blame, imply a remedy, or commit the business without the right authority.

Publishing

One transcript became five assets overnight.

Now there are quotes, posts, show notes, sponsor lines, and newsletter copy, each carrying its own source and review burden.

Operations

The packet is almost enough. That is the dangerous part.

Enough to act is not always enough to defend. Shipment, provider, support, and activation work often fail in that gap.

The simple answer

One work object in. One Move Pack out.

A Move Pack is a short, reviewable decision packet. It tells the team what the work is trying to become and what must be true before it moves.

The output is not the product.

The reviewed movement decision is the product. Good Remedy does not replace judgment. It makes the judgment point visible.

SupportedWhat is backed by source, policy, evidence, or prior decision.
MissingWhat is incomplete, assumed, unsupported, or unclear.
ReviewerWho must review before market, client, leadership, operations, or automation.
PostureMove, revise, hold, escalate, reject, or authority required.
Next actionThe smallest useful step that makes the work safer and clearer.
Public sales lanes

The companies are not the lane. The work shape is the lane.

Each lane names the work category, the movement risk, and the readiness function. The proof examples sit behind the cards.

Campaigns

Advertising and Brand Readiness

Ads, landing pages, public copy, creative briefs, and campaign claims before launch or client review.

Proof pattern: public campaign review
Client comms

Client Communication Readiness

Updates, recommendations, apologies, service recovery, and escalation notes before send.

Proof pattern: client email readiness
Publishing

Media and Publishing Readiness

Transcripts, quotes, show notes, newsletters, social copy, sponsor copy, and derivatives.

Proof pattern: media transcript packet
Activation

Privacy-Sensitive Activation Readiness

Audience packages, data signals, partner roles, buyer claims, and permission basis.

Proof pattern: synthetic activation packet
Retail ops

Retail Vendor and Shipment Readiness

PO, BOL, appointments, carrier notes, exceptions, evidence, and dispute routing.

Proof pattern: genericized shipment packet
Provider payer

Provider and Payer Workflow Readiness

Documentation, appeals, authorization support, source requirements, and follow-up packets.

Proof pattern: genericized provider workflow
Service

Customer Service and Response Readiness

Chatbot answers, support macros, complaint replies, refund language, and escalation notes.

Proof pattern: service recovery and auto-send
Leadership

Executive and Board Update Readiness

Vendor AI recommendations, performance readouts, strategic memos, and budget narratives.

Proof pattern: board update scenario
AI resource control

AI can create leverage, or it can create hidden operating cost.

Good Remedy is not anti AI. Good Remedy makes AI use accountable enough to budget, govern, and improve.

Prompt sprawl

Repeated AI cycles without a clear next decision.

Review burden

Human cleanup work that grows because the first movement rule was unclear.

Reuse gap

Useful work gets recreated instead of turned into a managed pattern.

Stop criteria

Some work should pause for source, owner, reviewer, or escalation instead of another model pass.

Boundary

Good Remedy prepares the work. Proper authority decides what moves.

Not certification

A Move Pack is a review packet, not a legal, clinical, regulatory, or final authority conclusion.

Not autopilot

AI and vendor outputs do not authorize action. The proper owner remains responsible for movement.

Not a black box

The packet shows support, gaps, reviewer lanes, release posture, and the next safe action.

Start here

Start with one non sensitive work object.

Send one draft, vendor recommendation, campaign note, client communication, workflow packet, or leadership update. Good Remedy returns a Move Pack showing movement posture, what must change, who must review, and the next safe action.
